response.justification: The candidate is a strong, versatile senior engineer, but his career centers on web and full-stack development (TypeScript, Node.js, DLang) rather than the deep, current C++ expertise this role requires. His only professional C++ experience was a brief 6-month stint at National Instruments in 2014–2015, C++ is absent from his skills list, and he shows no exposure to MQTT, IEC 62443, SDL practices, or industrial automation protocols. While his low-latency DLang work demonstrates transferable systems skills (async I/O, concurrency, high-throughput services), the gap to the role's core stack and industrial-cybersecurity requirements is too large for a senior/mentoring position.
strengths: Proven high-performance systems work: low-latency services handling millions of requests/sec with async I/O, fibers, and caching
Broad engineering craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, event-driven architectures, and experience mentoring/teaching (workshops, courses)
Long, diverse track record (15+ years) spanning backend, embedded-adjacent, and platform development
concerns: Minimal professional C++ experience (one ~6-month role in 2014–2015); C++ not listed in current skills
No evidence of IEC 62443, Secure Development Lifecycle, MQTT, or industrial-automation protocol experience
Recent focus is web/full-stack and AI tooling, far from networked multi-threaded C++ platform development

key_factors.factor: No industrial cybersecurity or IEC 62443 background
